title: A City Without Your Name
theme: Relocation, starting over, homesickness, and building belonging in a new environment.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: INT. NEW APARTMENT — 6:30 PM. Boxes line the walls. The apartment feels unfamiliar.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: NOAH, 35, stands at the window. Cars pass below. Thousands of people. Not one knows his name.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: He chose to move here.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Then why does he look lonely?

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Because choosing change and adjusting to change aren't the same thing.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Coffee shops. Supermarkets. City streets. Everywhere feels temporary.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Nobody talks to him.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Nobody knows him yet.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Is there a difference?

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: A big one. Not being known yet is temporary. Not being seen at all is something else.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Clue added — starting over from zero.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: A busy park. Families. Friends. Couples. Noah sits alone on a bench. Watching.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: He's observing everyone else's life.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Because he hasn't built one here yet.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: How long does that take?

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Longer than most people expect. Research suggests it takes an average of three months to make a casual friend in a new place. Six months for something real.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: That's a long time to feel invisible.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: It is.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Clue added — feeling invisible.

intro_dialogue.speaker: narration
intro_dialogue.text: Back in the apartment. Noah opens social media.

observation_dialogue.speaker: player
observation_dialogue.text: Everyone back home is moving forward.

observation_dialogue.speaker: avery
observation_dialogue.text: Or at least that's what the feed shows.

observation_dialogue.speaker: player
observation_dialogue.text: Still. He's here alone and they're all there together.

observation_dialogue.speaker: avery
observation_dialogue.text: Distance makes the highlight reel hit differently.

dialogue.speaker: narration
dialogue.text: Noah puts the phone down. Stares at the ceiling.

dialogue.speaker: player
dialogue.text: He looks like he regrets moving.

dialogue.speaker: avery
dialogue.text: Not yet. But the doubt is starting.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Saturday. No plans. No invitations. No routine.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: This is harder than work.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Because work provides structure. The weekend just... opens up. And when you have no one to fill it with, it can feel endless.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: So being busy at work actually hides the loneliness?

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Until Saturday. Then there's nowhere to hide.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Clue added — difficulty adjusting to a new routine.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Noah stays longer than necessary. Orders a second coffee. Then a third.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: He doesn't even want coffee.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: He wants company.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: So he sits in a coffee shop pretending to need caffeine.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: He's seeking proximity. Being near people even without interacting with them. It takes the edge off.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: But it's not real connection.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: No. But it's a start.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Clue added — seeking proximity without connection.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Messages from old friends. A reunion. Vacation plans. Noah starts typing. Stops. Deletes.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Why not send it?

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Because he doesn't know where he belongs anymore.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: He could just say he misses them.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: That would require admitting that the move hasn't been what he hoped. And he's not ready for that yet.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: So he says nothing.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Silence during transition is one of the most common — and most isolating — responses.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Clue added — silence during transition.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: His sister calls. They talk for forty minutes. After the call, the apartment feels quieter.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: The call helped.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: And hurt.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: It reminded him what belonging feels like. And then the call ended.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: So it made the silence louder.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: That's homesickness. It's not missing a place. It's missing a feeling.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Clue added — homesickness.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: A community photography course. Noah almost leaves. Instead, he stays.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: This feels important.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: It is.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Because he chose discomfort over avoidance. That's not a small thing.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: He doesn't know anyone in there.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: He didn't know anyone here three months ago either. But he's still here.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Clue added — taking social risks.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: The coffee shop barista recognizes him.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: BARISTA: Usual order?

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Noah smiles. For the first time.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: That's a small thing.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Belonging starts with small things.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Someone knowing his order.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Someone knowing he exists. Regularly. Reliably. That's the beginning of being known.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Clue added — beginning to feel known.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Several months later. Same city. Same coffee shop. Different Noah.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: He's sitting with friends. Laughing.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: The city finally knows his name.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: And he finally feels like he belongs.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: It took a while.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: It always does. Belonging isn't something a city gives you. It's something you build — one coffee order, one class, one conversation at a time.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Let's see if we understood how.

scenes.title: The New City
scenes.instructions: Explore the city. Visit locations to discover opportunities for connection. Each place holds something.

locations.label: Apartment
locations.opportunity: Rest and recharge. But don't stay too long.
locations.insight: Home is important — but belonging requires going beyond it.

locations.label: Coffee Shop
locations.opportunity: A barista who remembers your name. A regular seat. A familiar face.
locations.insight: Small repeated interactions are the foundation of belonging.

locations.label: Library
locations.opportunity: A reading group meets every Thursday. The sign is easy to miss.
locations.insight: Shared interests create natural entry points for connection.

locations.label: Park
locations.opportunity: A weekly outdoor yoga class. Free. Open to everyone.
locations.insight: Regular shared spaces create the conditions for friendship.

locations.label: Community Center
locations.opportunity: Photography course. Cooking class. Board game nights. Three ways in.
locations.insight: Community centers exist specifically to help people find each other.

scenes.title: Connection Chain
scenes.instructions: Link the events in the correct order to show how friendships grow.

chains.title: The Coffee Shop Path
chains.explanation: Belonging through consistency — showing up repeatedly until you become a familiar face.

chains.title: The Class Path
chains.explanation: Belonging through shared activity — doing something together creates natural connection.

chains.title: The Neighbour Path
chains.explanation: Belonging through proximity — the people closest to you physically can become closest to you emotionally.

scenes.title: Roots of Belonging
scenes.instructions: Tap positive actions to grow roots. Avoid negative ones — they weaken the tree.

scenes.title: Chapter 4 — Noah Complete
scenes.summary: Noah moved to a city where no one knew his name. He sat in parks watching other people's lives. He ordered extra coffees just to stay near people. He almost left a photography class before it began. Belonging didn't come all at once. It came through showing up — again and again — until the city started to feel like his.
keyLearnings: Choosing to relocate and adjusting to relocation are two different challenges.
Feeling invisible in a new place is a normal and temporary part of the process.
Belonging is built through repeated small interactions, not single big moments.
Homesickness is about missing a feeling of connection, not just a physical place.
Taking social risks — even small ones — is how new roots begin to grow.

scoreUpdate.emotionalAwareness: based on connection chain and roots performance
